venue.name: Monaco

ch.showdown.smgp.confirmedEvent: Almanac tunnel duel: Senna and Ceara nose-to-tail in the dark with D.P. on the line, both kissing Armco within a breath of each other on the last lap; Senna took the flag (what-really-happened.json:235); champion of record A. Senna / Madonna (:232). Template fires while the live showdown is undecided, so art is result-free.
ch.showdown.smgp.tone: knife-edge; a held breath; the argument the grandstand never stops having
requiredVisible: red-and-gold #1 and gunmetal #17 nose-to-tail in tunnel shadow

dnf.mechanical.confirmedEvent: Season-5 smoke plague: retirements at an all-time record, the treeline at West Germany eating a season of engines whole in one scorching weekend (seasons.json:269,280,296); failures mechanical not human, drivers coast to the grass and walk back to applause (seasons.json:265).
dnf.mechanical.weather: scorching heat (authored for the reference weekend, seasons.json:280)
dnf.mechanical.tone: cruel luck; industrial pathos; the walked-away applause
requiredVisible: coasted car on grass at the treeline trailing smoke and steam
anonymous driver walking away, rear view

mt.transfer.smgp.venueNote: No track scene: Bullets backstreet garage and paddock, winter preseason signing and season-end dissolution.
mt.transfer.smgp.confirmedEvent: Winter signing of the one-season works-engine deal, the only time the backstreet garage ever runs establishment power (seasons.json:467); dissolution under establishment pressure at season's end, Ceara shakes the engineers' hands and goes back to building his own thunder (seasons.json:474; hooks 485,493).
mt.transfer.smgp.weather: winter authored for the signing; scene is indoors so weather is immaterial
mt.transfer.smgp.tone: cold-war paddock politics; a deal too good to last; defiant craft pride
requiredVisible: gunmetal #17 nose with brass blade under a work lamp
two silhouetted figures in handshake or paperwork exchange, no faces
winter light through the garage door

aspectRatios.safeZone: car nose left-of-center and handshake right-of-center, both inside the central 40% x 50% core

smgp-dispatch:milestone.first-win.confirmedEvent: Brazil champion of record G. Ceara / Bullets (what-really-happened.json:22); authored rain coronation: the heavens opened on lap one and the Bullets simply vanished up the road, the grandees paddling in his spray (:26).
smgp-dispatch:milestone.first-win.tone: coronation in the rain; a home fortress roaring; the wet master's apotheosis
requiredVisible: gunmetal #17 with brass blade and green flash vanishing up a wet bumpy straight, rear three-quarter view
pursuing grandees as indistinct silhouettes in spray
forbiddenVisible: any maiden or first-win visual claim

smgp-dispatch:setback.died.venueNote: Venue-free pit-lane and garage memorial, by design; keeps clear of packet 2's Adelaide scene.
smgp-dispatch:setback.died.confirmedEvent: Authored voice: TRAGEDY. {player} has been killed in an accident here. The SMGP world loses one of its own, and the circus falls silent. (dispatches.json:123) with the mourning pool (:29-35).
smgp-dispatch:setback.died.timeOfDay: UNKNOWN (grey stilled light is a palette choice, labeled as such, not canon)
smgp-dispatch:setback.died.tone: restrained mourning: silence, flags at half-mast, the empty seat; never graphic
requiredVisible: flags at half-mast over a pit lane (pool verbatim)
forbiddenVisible: crash depiction, wreckage, body, flames, gore
driver identity, portrait, or the deceased's helmet
any implication of a canon death
resemblance to a real person's memorial or documentary photograph

smgp-season-lore:1.confirmedEvent: Gen-3 homologation freeze becomes law (seasons.json:22); stewards declare the featherweight Bullets legal, Dardan's protest fails (:41); the King's arithmetic read aloud at San Marino (:44); season opens at Imola, closes at Monaco (pack calendar). Lore declares no champion; the sim decides.
smgp-season-lore:1.weather: Clear (pack-authored for every season-1 session; packs/smgp-1/season.json weatherSlots; docs/dev/smgp-design.md:46)
smgp-season-lore:1.timeOfDay: UNKNOWN (neutral clear daylight is an art choice, not canon)
smgp-season-lore:1.tone: stone-carved old order at full weight; a serene king; a quiet, almost unnoticed beginning
requiredVisible: red-and-gold #1 car by number and palette, no likeness
season-opener pit-straight atmosphere at Imola, generic furniture only
muted Iron Circus palette with one imperial red-and-gold accent
forbiddenVisible: Senna face, helmet, or McLaren-era trade dress
150th-GP Silverstone motif and its awe-silent grandstand
pace cars, medical corps, rebuilt Charter barriers

aspectRatios.safeZone: subject in central 40% width x 50% height; nothing load-bearing in outer 20% on any edge

provenanceConcerns: highest likeness risk in the set (real, deceased public figure); abstraction mandatory

venue.name: Monaco (finale)
venue.frameNote: Charter-era state: relit tunnel, rebuilt barriers, pace-car fixture, silver medical car (seasons.json:1036,584)

smgp-season-lore:17.confirmedEvent: The twenty-sixth championship proclaimed the Crown of Crowns, sixteen rounds staged as chapters of one finale (seasons.json:1033,1038,1068); Senna's eve-of-San-Marino declaration (:1046); the last flag falls at Monaco (:1031,1075); the sealed prize beyond the last flag for the campaign's finishers (:1042,1079). Lore declares no champion; the sim decides.
smgp-season-lore:17.timeOfDay: UNKNOWN (relit tunnel is a permanent state; farewell warmth is palette, not canon)
smgp-season-lore:17.tone: valedictory, reverent, conclusive; pilgrims at the crown jewel; a sealed mystery beyond the flag
requiredVisible: crown-jewel street circuit in farewell dress: Armco, hairpin, tunnel exit glow (warm lit portal, not darkness)
one imperial red-and-gold #1 as the only car
Charter-era safety furniture permitted (rebuilt barriers, distant silver medical car)
muted Golden Circus palette with one jubilee gold accent
forbiddenVisible: heritage-liveries drift toward real trade dress
the sealed prize depicted (special.jpg and ultimate.jpg are sealed finale assets)

aspectRatios.safeZone: subject in central 40% width x 50% height

provenanceConcerns: jubilee pageantry stays inside SMGP fictional palettes
contradictions: ordinal-16 vs ordinal-17 motif conflation caught and corrected (heritage liveries are the Silver Jubilee's, seasons.json:967-1026)

era.inWorldGeneration: peak Gen-4, the final angriest evolution (seasons.json:519)

venue.name: Australia
venue.frameNote: pre-Charter scene: no pace car, no medical corps, no rebuilt barriers

smgp-season-lore:9.confirmedEvent: The Second Great Wall Sunday: half the field in the Armco, Gould out-dared at last, the tannoy falling silent over a standing grandstand; everyone walks away, and no one pretends nothing happened (seasons.json:520,533); the silent-grandstand photograph becomes the era's full stop (:549); the Safety Charter announced at Monaco: pace-car law, pit-lane speed limits, rebuilt barriers, standing medical corps, twenty percent power cut (:537,557); the retirement column ran longer than the finishing order (:556).
smgp-season-lore:9.weather: UNKNOWN (none authored for the Australia scene)
smgp-season-lore:9.tone: the somber hinge of the whole campaign: attrition, silence, conscience, then reform
requiredVisible: empty concrete-walled street circuit (Adelaide walls, canon-sanctioned)
standing silent grandstand as a hushed mass silhouette
no cars on the racing surface
forbiddenVisible: crash depiction, wreckage, debris, bodies, flames, car smoke
Charter furniture at the Australia scene (pace car, silver medical car, rebuilt barriers)
the awe-silent grandstand of Senna's 150th (this packet owns the motif, grief register only)

aspectRatios.safeZone: subject in central 40% width x 50% height

provenanceConcerns: fatality-adjacent material under the stricter ART-P3-001 review
must not resemble documentary photography of any real crash
contradictions: almanac's earlier Great Wall Sunday shows a crawling pace car (what-really-happened.json:221) while standing pace-car law arrives only with the 1998 Charter (seasons.json:537); handled by showing the 1998 scene car-free
